source_jobs.title: Configuration Management Analyst
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a family of SBA-certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified companies delivering IT, management consulting, and general construction solutions to the Federal Government and commercial sector. They are actively seeking a Configuration Management Analyst based in Crystal City, Arlington, VA. This is a full time, hybrid role. This position requires an active DoD Secret/SAR Clearance.

This is an exciting opportunity to be part of the team supporting the F-35/JPO project. The minimum requirements for this role are:

 - Experience in program management, technical or business analysis discipline; a minimum of four years out of the prior eight years of program experience includes aircraft systems configuration management.

 - Demonstrated program experience in the configuration management process must include: configuration identification, control, audit, and status; DOD configuration management policies, procedures, review cycles, instructions and standards; engineering change proposal evaluations.

 - Demonstrated CM experience with development, production, ancillary equipment, ground support equipment, test & evaluation, and fleet operations of a DoD and international acquisition aircraft program.

 - Experience evaluating Major Variance Requests, Specification Change Notices, Engineering Change Requests/Proposals, Manufacturing Build Records, Sustainment as Maintained Records and other documents.

 - Demonstrated experience in providing guidance and assistance in configuration management, and change control and ensuring that all Class I.

 - Education: Master’s Degree in Engineering, S&T, Business Management or related discipline Or a Bachelor’s Degree plus 4 years additional work experience related to the labor category functional description.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

source_jobs.title: Engineering Technicians
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a family of SBA-certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified companies delivering IT, management consulting, and general construction solutions to the Federal Government and commercial sector.

They are actively seeking a Engineering Technicians based in Colorado Springs, CO. This is a full-time, onsite position. This position requires an active Top Secret clearance with the ability to obtain Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI).



 - Engineering Technician 5 (Range $40-$45) (7) years of experience

 - Engineering Technician 4 (Range $30-$35) (5) years of experience

This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a team supporting a USSC NIWC contract in Colorado Springs, CO.

The main responsibilities of the role include:

 - Use hand and power tools to solder and crimp connectors along with various other IT and AV systems related tasks.

 - Cabling installation of copper cable up to CAT6A and SM/MM Fiber

 - Termination of copper cable up to CAT6A and SM/MM Fiber to MPO/MTP

 - IT and AV rack installation and Wiring

 - Projector and screen support structure installations.

 - Mounting LCD/LED displays and video walls.

 - Test point to point functionality of systems.

 - Testing and recording of all cables.

 - Travel to various job sites around Colorado Springs area.

 - Candidates with experience installing systems on government sites will be rated more highly.

 - Position requires a background check to include a DMV report, current and valid driver’s license.

 - Other duties assigned as needed.

Skills and Abilities

 - Effectively communicate with employees, customers, and colleagues.

 - Ability to use hand and power tools in a safe and efficient manner.

 - Ability to work and think independently and ensure to meet deadlines.

 - Read and interpret electronic schematics and architectural blueprints.

Education and/or Experience

 - Minimum audio-visual installation experience required.

 - Formal education in electronics or related field preferred.

 - Minimum of a High School Diploma or equivalent required.

External Training

 - Avixa CTS and/or CTS-I certification, required.

 - BICSI certification required.

 - Vendor (Extron/AMX/Crestron/Biamp/Planar, CommScope, Corning, etc.) certification preferred.

Clearance Requirement

Technicians will be required to obtain SCI Government Security Clearance, most currently have Top Secret Clearance.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. They are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. They are committed to a collaborative, inclusive environment that encourages authenticity and fosters a sense of belonging. We are also committed to providing accommodations for persons with disabilities.

source_jobs.title: Junior Server Administrator
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a family of SBA-certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified companies delivering IT, management consulting, and general construction solutions to the Federal Government and commercial sector.

They are actively seeking a Junior Server Administrator. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of our client's dynamic team supporting a Team Awareness Kit Interagency program.

Clearance: Public Trust background investigation is required for this position.

Workplace Type: This position will be staffed as full-time equivalent (FTE). Work will be completed on-site at a government facility in Washington DC.

Compensation: $60,000 - $75,000 per year

The main responsibilities of the role include:

 - Maintain Server installation and operating system updates; Track, manage and sustain Platform Server modifications, updates, modifications, patches; Provide Server and/or Platform support for customers.

 - Identify, propose, manage, install, configure, sustain and support third party (integrated) application required to support the platform. Tactical Awareness Kit Interagency Operability USSS - Standard Sensitive Info.

 - Manage and maintain all patches to ensure Security Compliance Provide technical support for application, appliance, device support and/or troubleshooting of the platform, third party applications associated to the platform and end user devices/appliances.

 - Coordinate with, and support Application Development teams working within the USSS for updates required for platform functionality.

 - Coordinate with and support Mobile Device Management teams withing within the USSS needed for platform, application and/or end user appliances.

 - Support installations, diagnosis, updates and sustainment of enterprise workstations. • Support the development of system administration policy, requirements and daily maintenance procedures and support activities.

 - Support the creation of maintenance plans, operational procedures, how to guidance, program documentation used by support technicians, users, help desk etc.

 - Support the integrate platform servers in small team and mission planning systems upon request by agency program managers.

 - Provide recommendations and support efforts to coordinate capability enhancements developed by the Department of Defense to receive operational approvals, certifications, and internal agency approvals needed for implementation.

 - Document newly identified mission cap based on emerging technology improvements/enhances and submit to agency program managers for review and potential integration to the current platform.

Qualifications:

 - Expertise Level: Junior

 - Experience: 1 year IT Server Administration experience.

 - Experience Needed: Azure Cloud computing, Linux Environments (Red Hat / CentOS), Windows Environments, VMware Environments, Network Security, Mobile Applications (iOS / Android), Mobile Device Management, Integrated and tactical Radio Networks, Geospatial / Mapping caching servers.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. They are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. source_jobs.title: Armed Security Guard

They are actively seeking Armed Security Guards (CSG) based in Umatilla County, Oregon. This position is onsite and provides support 24 hours a day/ 7 days a week, 365 days a year. Scheduled shifts will be 12 hours. Pre employment drug, medical screen and Physical Ability Test (PAT) will be required.

This is an exciting opportunity to be part of our client’s team supporting their efforts at a facility on the Columbia River. The main responsibilities of the role include:

 - Overall security and safety of the facility, activities, and security awareness.

 - Provide security services on-site.

 - Conduct foot and/or vehicle patrols.

 - Utilization of tour tracking software in the performance of daily duties.

 - Collaboration with local law enforcement agencies when necessary.

 - Continuous physical effort that requires prolonged periods of walking, standing.

 - Occasional lifting and carrying up to 50 pounds.



Education and Certification Requirements:

 - Possession of state Guard Card certification.

 - Possession of a valid Driver’s License.

 - Tier 3 adjudicated investigation.

 - Weapons Qualifications: Both Oregon and Washington Qualifications.

source_jobs.title: Installation Technician
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a family of SBA-certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified companies delivering IT, management consulting, and general construction solutions to the Federal Government and commercial sector. They are actively seeking Installation Technicians to support the FAA LIFT Program by performing installation, testing, and documentation of electronic systems and equipment at FAA operational facilities nationwide. The technicians will ensure that all work is completed in accordance with FAA engineering drawings, specifications, standards, and safety regulations while minimizing operational downtime and maintaining system integrity.

Position Title: Installation Technician – LIFT Program

Reports To: EPJV Project Manager / FAA Electronics Crew Chief

Location: FAA Western Service Area (WSA) States - Alaska, Arizona, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, Washington, Wyoming.

Employment Type: Full-Time / Rotational (100% Travel Required)

Compensation: $40 - 50 per hour plus per diem.

Duties and Responsibilities:

 - Perform field installation, relocation, and removal of FAA electronic equipment and supporting infrastructure.

 - Coordinate schedules with FAA SSC, ES, and Air Traffic personnel.

 - Review and interpret engineering drawings, schematics, specifications, and transmittals.

 - Identify and communicate project material and equipment needs in advance of installation dates.

 - Conduct site preparation and verify adequacy of space, power, grounding, and communications paths.

 - Establish communications between control and remote sites, ensuring full data and signal integrity.

 - Perform end-to-end signal testing, data collection, and documentation for Technical and FRD records.

 - Adhere to FAA standards, including but not limited to FAA-STD-019, FAA-STD-020, FAA-C-1217, FAA Order 6510, FAA Order 6580.3A, FAA Order 6480.6B, and the NEC/NFPA Codes.

 - Generate and update drawings, sketches, reports, and project documentation.

 - Participate in project briefings, out-briefs, and resolution of on-site issues or exceptions.

 - Support NAS TechOps in returning systems to service after scheduled outages.

 - Maintain strict compliance with FAA safety, security, and operational requirements at all times.

Qualifications and Requirements

 - Minimum of 5 years of experience performing installation of electronic, communications, or control systems, preferably within FAA or similar critical infrastructure environments.

 - Strong understanding of FAA installation standards, electrical work, grounding/bonding, and communication systems.

 - Ability to interpret and execute engineering documentation, drawings, and project specifications.

 - Demonstrated ability to coordinate with multiple FAA stakeholders (SSC, ES, Air Traffic).

 - Proficiency in data collection, testing, and documentation procedures.

 - Possess or able to obtain required state or federal licenses/certifications where applicable.

 - Must be able to travel frequently (100%) and work at remote FAA sites for extended periods (2–3 weeks per assignment).

 - Must meet security clearance and access requirements for FAA facilities.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills; ability to generate concise reports and updates.

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Asbestos Operations & Maintenance (O&M)

 - Lead O&M / Lead Awareness

 - Fall Protection

 - First Aid / CPR / AED

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. They are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirm

source_jobs.title: Quality Control Site Supervisor
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a multifaceted Class A General Contractor that is focused on government construction. Their team has extensive experience in all aspects of construction, including roofing, environmental management, engineering services, and manufacturing. The client’s objective at the end of each project is for the client to feel like their team has exceeded their expectations – that they received a superior product of exceptional value that was delivered on time and within the established budget.

They are seeking an experienced Quality Control Site Supervisor to coordinate with and assist the Project Manager, vendors, subcontractors and management to determine the need for manpower, materials, and equipment. The QC Site Supervisor will be the direct liaison with the Government Contracting Official as well as directing the daily activities of subcontractors and vendors to ensure compliance with contract documents, safety rules, and quality/security requirements to maximize production and eliminate disruptions and downtime. Ensures the construction schedule and budget are kept in accordance to plan.

Workplace Type: Full-Time, Onsite. Virginia Beach, VA.

Compensation: $90 – 100K per year.

Responsibilities:

· Maintains high level of professionalism and carries out a policy of courtesy and superior customer service. Exercises tact, good manners and diplomacy.

· Maintains a thorough understanding of commercial construction industry practices and standards.

· Accomplishes construction project results by defining project purpose and scope; calculating resources required; establishing standards and protocols; allocating resources; scheduling and coordinating staff and sub-contractors; evaluating milestone assumptions and conclusions; resolving design problems; evaluating and implementing change orders.

· Meets construction budget by monitoring project expenditures; identifying variances; implementing corrective actions; providing non-project annual operating and capital budget information.

· Supervises the coordination and input as needed with the contractors providing services and infrastructure.

· Meets operational standards by contributing construction information to strategic plans and reviews; implementing production, productivity, quality, and customer service standards; resolving problems; identifying construction management system improvements.

· Manages sub-contractors by locating, evaluating, and selecting sub-contractors; monitors, controls performance, and ensures quality workmanship.

· Oversees preparation of daily logs and reports of construction activity for office submittal.

· Supervises change proposals, proposal requests, requests for information; supplemental instructions and other contract documentation are required on our client’s behalf.

· Ensures each subcontractor is provided with subcontract package and all agreements, submittals, required government documents, W-9, Certificate of Insurance, Certified Payroll, Lien Waivers, Schedule of Values, etc. are signed and returned to client’s headquarters.

· Maintains safe, secure, and healthy work environment by following and enforcing standards and procedures to complying with legal regulations and OSHA standards.

· Schedules and conducts required Pre-Construction Contractor Quality Control (CQC) and safety meetings with every individual on the jobsite.

· Communicates progress of projects to the Project Manager and ensures deadlines are met.

· Provides coordination and input as needed with contractors providing services and infrastructure for the project including schedule and cost management.

· Monitors and ensures schedule performance and quality workmanship of contractors.

· Prepares and submits daily log and reports of construction activities to requiring authority.

· Reviews change proposals, proposal requests, requests for information, s

source_jobs.title: Integration and Test Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Strategic Growth Partners provides both operations & strategic support to tribal 8(a) & commercial organizations. Our client we are supporting is a family of companies are comprised of multiple SBA Certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified organizations focused on delivering Information Technology, Management Consulting, and General Construction solutions to the Federal Government along with the Commercial Sector. They are actively seeking an Integration and Test Systems Engineer based in Orlando, FL. This is a full-time onsite opportunity. This position requires an active Secret Clearance.

Clearance: Active DoD Secret Clearance

Workplace Type: Onsite, Orlando, FL.

Compensation: $82 – 93K per year.

This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a great team supporting developing software for emerging capabilities for the Mobility Air Force Distributed Mission Operations program. The capability to train the Aerial Refueling Mission is important for the success of the program, and will require innovative analytical and integration test tools. The main responsibilities of the role include:

• Performs technical planning, system integration, verification and validation, cost and risk, and supportability and effectiveness analyses for total systems.

• Analyses are performed at all levels of total system product to include concept, design, fabrication, test, installation, operation, and maintenance.

• Ensures the logical and systematic conversion of customer or product requirements into total systems solutions that acknowledge technical, schedule, and cost constraints.

• Performs functional analysis, timeline analysis, detail trade studies, requirements allocation and interface definition studies to translate customer requirements into hardware and software specifications.

Duties include but are not limited to:

• Provide technical support to Coalition and MAJCOM training exercises.

• Participate in software requirements development.

• Review/write/update test plans/procedures and ensure they meet the objectives of the contractual requirements and participates in the testing process to validate requirements.

QUALIFICATIONS:

• 3 - 4 years relevant experience with Bachelors in related field; 0 years experience with Masters in related field; or High School Diploma or equivalent and 6 years relevant experience.

• Familiar with basic scripting and software development concepts.

• Prior software/hardware testing, analysis, and troubleshooting experience.

• Must be a US citizen, able to secure and maintain a Secret Security Clearance.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

• Previous experience with MAF or other Distributed Training Networks and systems.

• Experience with NGTS, other Computer-Generated Forces tools, simulated radios.

• Analysis and troubleshooting of data in DIS/HLA simulation environments/networks.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. They are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. They are committed to a collaborative, inclusive environment that encourages authenticity and fosters a sense of belonging. They are also committed to providing accommodations for persons with disabilities.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accounting Manager / Controller
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting is proud to support a valued client recognized as a premium-quality contractor specializing in the design, integration, and installation of cutting-edge IT infrastructure systems. Our client is committed to delivering high-bandwidth, secure, mobile, and unified communications solutions that meet the evolving needs of commercial and government sectors alike. With a focus on reliability, scalability, and innovation, they empower organizations to achieve seamless connectivity and superior performance across mission-critical environments.

They are seeking an experienced Senior Accounting Manager / Controller to lead and own the company’s accounting function end-to-end. This role is responsible for timely month-end close, accurate financial reporting, internal controls, audit readiness, and hands-on leadership of day-to-day accounting operations.

This is a high-impact role for a detail-oriented accounting leader who thrives in fast-paced environments, values accountability, and can bring structure, discipline, and visibility to the finance organization.

Responsibilities:

Financial Close & Reporting

 - Own and execute monthly, quarterly, and year-end close with established timelines.

 - Ensure accurate and GAAP-compliant financial statements (P&L, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow).

 - Prepare and review journal entries, accruals, and account reconciliations.

 - Deliver timely financial packages and variance analysis to leadership.

Accounting Operations

 - Oversee AP, AR, payroll coordination, expense reporting, and general ledger.

 - Ensure accuracy and completeness of revenue recognition and job costing.

 - Maintain chart of accounts, accounting policies, and documentation.

 - Partner with project managers and operations on financial accuracy and controls.

Internal Controls & Compliance

 - Establish and maintain strong internal controls and close checklists.

 - Support external auditors, tax advisors, and third-party providers.

 - Ensure compliance with applicable federal, state, and local requirements.

 - Improve processes to reduce risk, errors, and rework.

Systems & Process Improvement

 - Serve as accounting system owner (ERP / accounting software).

 - Identify inefficiencies and implement scalable processes and automation.

 - Improve reporting accuracy, close speed, and visibility into financial data..

Leadership & Accountability

 - Provide hands-on leadership and oversight of accounting staff.

 - Set clear expectations, deadlines, and performance standards.

 - Train, mentor, and develop team members while holding them accountable.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with operations, HR vendors, and leadership.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental, and vision insurance coverage; 11 company-paid holidays per year; and paid time off.

source_jobs.title: Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a multifaceted Class A General Contractor that is focused on government construction. Their team has extensive experience in all aspects of construction, including roofing, environmental management, engineering services, and manufacturing. The client's objective at the end of each project is for the client to feel like their team has exceeded their expectations that they received a superior product of exceptional value that was delivered on time and within the established budget.

They are seeking an experienced Operations Manager who oversees the daily activities of Site Superintendents, Project Managers, and Administrative staff at their Quantico locations and is ultimately responsible for the overall productivity of the projects being done.

Workplace Type: Full-Time, Onsite. Quantico, VA.

Compensation: $100 – 120K per year.

Responsibilities:

· Demonstrate professionalism and uphold a policy of superior customer service.

· Define project purpose and scope; calculating resources required; establish standards and protocols; allocating resources; schedule and coordinate staff and sub-contractors; evaluating milestone assumptions and conclusions; resolving design problems; assess and implement change orders.

· Conduct project inspections at critical phases; obtaining approvals from customers.

· Keep confidentiality of sensitive information.

· Update job knowledge by participating in educational opportunities.

· Government contracting experience required. Experience with JOC or IDIQ contracts preferred.

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant
source_jobs.description:
Strategic Growth Partners is committed to upholding our company values and ensuring data confidentiality. We provide robust services to our clients across multiple time zones and foster a collaborative, innovative, and diverse work environment.

Strategic Growth Partners (SGP) is seeking an experienced Senior Accountant to join our finance team. This position can be remote or in person or a hybrid. Clients are located all over the USA. We don’t expect regular travel for this position, although there might be an occasional work trip to visit a client. There is an expectation to be onsite in the office in Newark, CA during the onboarding and training for this position, and one some regular cadence if candidate is not based near the office.

Workplace Type: Remote/Hybrid, Newark, CA.

Compensation: W-2. $80,000 - $120,000 per year.

Key Responsibilities:

· An ideal candidate can jump in and hit the ground running immediately.

· Someone who can support multiple client companies and be able to effectively change context. throughout the day, and still maintain professional work quality.

· Maintain client books on a real time basis.

· Prepares financial statements under US GAAP.

· Assists in compliance with developing controls, policies, and procedures.

· Experience supporting Federal Contracts and familiar with DCAA, FAR Clauses, Cost Accounting, and/or Construction Projects is a major plus.

· Liaise with HR on all payroll related matters.

· Collaborate with other SGP divisions on special projects.

· Provide information to external accountants for audit, tax return preparation.

· Coordinate with other client company partners (vendors, insurance carriers, etc.).

· Work with Surety for bonding facility companies etc.

· Effective and timely communication.

· Well organized, structured, and prepared to work with un-organized client situations.

· Provide thoughtful recommendations that are backed by data and analysis.



Assist COO, CFO, Client Managers and Controller in:

· Budget preparation, monitoring, and performance measurement on a regular basis.

· Tracking projects and provide critical analysis of project costs.

· Support in cash flow management.

· Compliance with federal, state, and local legal requirements.

· Liaise with service providers for accounting purposes.

· Preparing Board reports.



Requirements:

· Minimum 6 years of relevant experience. Must have at least 3 years of experience working with Government Contracts across a variety of industries like IT, Construction, Defense, etc.

· Must have hands on experience and an ability to train junior team members on DCAA, FARs, CAS, and other Federal Compliance standards and their impact on finance and accounting tasks.

· Ability to work in an open, fast-paced, office under high pressure and numerous deadlines.

· Ready for challenges. Knows how to prioritize work. Able to utilize new technology tools to manage and coordinate client tasks and projects.

· Knowledgeable with HR policies.

· Excellent written and oral communication skills.

· Demonstrated team management and interpersonal skills.

· Technology savvy and can handle video calls, emails, Microsoft Office Suite.

· Adaptable and willing to learn.

SGP is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. We are committed to a collaborative, inclusive environment that encourages authenticity and fosters a sense of belonging. We are also committed to providing accommodations for persons with disabilities.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Consultant
source_jobs.description:
Strategic Growth Partners is committed to upholding our company values and ensuring data confidentiality. We provide robust IT solutions to our clients across multiple time zones and foster a collaborative, innovative, and diverse work environment.

Strategic Growth Partners (SGP) is seeking a knowledgeable and client-focused Business Development Consultant to support our growth across federal, state, and local government markets with an emphasis on non-traditional contracting pathways. This is a 1099 independent contractor role centered on BD support and client relationship management.

The ideal candidate has familiarity with non-traditional contracting mechanisms including Commercial Solutions Openings (CSOs), Broad Agency Announcements (BAAs), SBIR/STTR programs, and grants, and brings established relationships across DoD, DHS, Intelligence Community, and/or civilian agency environments.

Workplace Type: Remote.

Compensation: 1099, $50.00 per hour.

Hours: Variable based on client activity and pipeline needs; hours agreed upon in advance.

Engagement: Initial contract term with option to extend based on performance and business need.

Travel: Occasional travel may be required for client meetings, agency visits, and industry events.

Key Responsibilities

Business Development Support

 - Support the identification and qualification of new business opportunities across federal, state, and local government, with a focus on non-traditional contracting vehicles (CSOs, BAAs, SBIR/STTRs, grants, OTAs, and similar mechanisms).

 - Monitor and track relevant solicitations, funding announcements, and agency priorities through SAM.gov http://SAM.gov, DSIP, grants.gov http://grants.gov, SBIR.gov http://SBIR.gov, and agency-specific portals.

 - Assist in developing BD strategies aligned to agency mission priorities and funding cycles.

 - Represent the company at industry days, conferences, agency outreach events, and government working groups.

 - Provide market intelligence and competitive landscape insights to inform go/no-go decisions and positioning.

Client Relationship Management

 - Serve as a primary client-facing representative, building and maintaining trusted relationships with agency program managers, contracting officers, technical leads, and other key stakeholders.

 - Conduct regular client touchpoints to understand evolving needs, priorities, and upcoming opportunities.

 - Communicate client feedback and agency intelligence back to internal teams to inform service delivery and BD strategy.

 - Support account growth by identifying follow-on opportunities and white space within existing client relationships.

 - Ensure a consistent, professional, and responsive experience for all clients across the engagement lifecycle.

Pipeline & Reporting

 - Maintain current and accurate opportunity tracking in CRM tools, including status, next steps, and key contacts.

 - Provide regular updates to leadership on BD activity, pipeline health, and market trends.

 - Track agency funding announcements, budget priorities, and procurement forecasts relevant to non-traditional pathways.

Required Qualifications

 - Demonstrated knowledge of non-traditional contracting mechanisms, including CSOs, BAAs, SBIR/STTR programs, federal and state grants, and Other Transaction Agreements (OTAs).

 - Strong networking, relationship-building, and communication skills; able to engage credibly with executives, program leadership, contracting officials, and technical stakeholders.

 - Experience supporting BD efforts across federal and state/local government clients

 - Working knowledge of agency procurement processes, funding cycles, and budget structures.

 - Self-directed and organized, with the ability to manage multiple client relationships and BD activities simultaneously.

 - Proficiency with SAM.gov http://SAM.gov, grants.gov http://grants.gov, SBIR.gov http://SBIR.gov, or similar platforms.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Direct experience

source_jobs.title: Project Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Strategic Growth Partners provides both operations & strategic support to tribal 8(a) & commercial organizations. Our client we are supporting is a premier provider of complex design and engineering services to the aerospace and defense industry. Additionally, they also provides program management support to many of its clients' R&D departments and is actively engaged in the development of several next generation platforms.

Our client is seeking a qualified Project Engineer to directly support the Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR), F/A-18 & EA-18G Fleet Support Team (FST) at Fleet Readiness Center Southwest (FRCSW) Naval Air Station (NAS) North Island, California. This is a full-time onsite opportunity. This position requires an active Secret Clearance.

Clearance: Active DoD Secret Clearance

Workplace Type: Onsite, San Diego, CA. NAS North Island.

Compensation: $55.00-$65.00 per hour

The main responsibilities of the role include:

· Determining project responsibilities by identifying project phases and elements and assigning personnel accordingly.

· Analyzing and establishing project specifications by studying product design, customer requirements, and performance standards.

· Designing and conducting technical studies and tests to confirm product performance and sustainment viability.

· Creating and managing detailed project schedules by calculating time requirements and sequencing project elements.

· Monitoring and coordinating ongoing project activities, identifying, and resolving issues to maintain project timelines.

· Controlling project plans through continuous review of designs, specifications, and schedule changes while recommending necessary actions to management.

· Preparing comprehensive project status reports, analyzing trends, and providing strategic recommendations.

· Responsible for planning and execution of engineering development efforts for a cross functional engineering and logistics team.

· Responsible for overseeing and managing complex engineering projects from conception to completion.

· Providing a blend of strong engineering expertise and proven project management skills to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.

· Serve as a key liaison for the FST lead and various stakeholders, including clients, engineering teams, and other departments, fostering effective communication and collaboration throughout the project lifecycle.

· Developing and maintaining project plans, staffing plans, and related planning documents to support FST tasking.

Minimum Experience Requirements:

· Has over three (3) to 10 years of experience in Engineering and Logistics program/project management.

· BS or BA degree in Business Administration or a "Relevant Technical Discipline".

· Must be able to sit and stand for extended periods, in an office environment.

Desired Experience:

· Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines. Degree in an engineering field is strongly preferred.

Our client provides equal employment opportunities for all prospective and current employees. They do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, protected veteran status, disability, genetic information, or any other status protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Senior Network Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a CVE-verified Veteran-Owned Small Business (VOSB) and a proud ISO 9001:2015 certified organization specializing in systems integration and enterprise management solutions. As a trusted GSA MAS contract holder, they deliver mission-critical telecommunications, network infrastructure, and IT services to Federal, State, and Local Government agencies, the U.S. Military, and private-sector clients.

They are seeking a Senior Network Engineer to join their team in Doral, FL. This position is on-site. Must have a current/active TS/SCI.

Employment - Full time

Worksite Type: Onsite

Compensation: $130,000 per year.

Security Clearance Required: Active TS/SCI

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

 - Plans, designs, and maintains the connectivity of enterprise network assets, ensuring compliance and secure operations on classified networks (SIPR, NIPR, JWICS).

 - Collaborates with stakeholders to address connectivity requirements and implements necessary configurations to facilitate efficient and secure data transmission.

 - Ensures all network switches and routers are operational, properly configured, and compliant with DISA STIG requirements; performs regular maintenance, updates, and troubleshooting.

 - Configures, maintains, and monitors firewalls, intrusion protection systems, intrusion detection systems, and Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) for network access control and authentication, and implements security measures for specialized systems and networks.

 - Supports the planning and implementation of enterprise service connections between networks, systems, and applications across domains, facilitating connections for auditing, authentication, secure cloud computing architecture (SCCA), and cross-domain solutions and guards.

 - Collaborates with ISSOs and Cybersecurity Services Leads in the Risk Management Framework (RMF) process, providing inputs and documentation, and mentors/trains other NOC staff and Junior Engineers.

Minimum Qualifications:

 - BA/BS in field necessary to assume Network Engineer or 4 years of additional experience in lieu of the degree

 - 9+ years of experience with 5+ years of relevant Network Engineer experience.

 - Deep understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, routing protocols) and network security principles.

 - Expertise in configuring and managing network devices, including routers, switches, firewalls, and intrusion detection/prevention systems, with hands-on experience with Cisco networking technologies (e.g., IOS, NX-OS) and security appliances (e.g., ASA firewalls, ISE).

 - Familiarity with network monitoring and management tools, knowledge of virtualization and cloud networking concepts, and experience with securing classified networks (SIPR, NIPR, JWICS) and implementing DISA STIGs.

 - Experience in cybersecurity frameworks and standards (e.g., NIST Cybersecurity Framework, RMF), knowledge of network access control and authentication mechanisms, ability to assess and mitigate network security risks, and DoD 8570.01-M IAT Level II certification.

Preferred Qualifications:

 - Master’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or a relevant discipline.

 - Experience with cross-domain solutions and guards. Knowledge of cloud computing security architectures (e.g., SCCA).

 - Experience with scripting and automation tools for network management. Familiarity with ITIL framework and service delivery concepts. ITIL Foundation certificate.

 - Relevant networking certifications (e.g., Cisco CCNP, CCIE, CompTIA Security+).

 - Experience at a DoD Combatant Command (e.g., SOUTHCOM, NORTHCOM, CENTCOM, CYBERCOM, INDOPACOM, EUCOM, AFRICOM, STRATCOM, TRANSCOM, SOCOM, SPACECOM) or a component is desired.

Physical Requirements:

 - Must be able to remain in stationary position for up to 50% of the time.

 - Must be able to deliver clear and effective co

source_jobs.title: Senior Virtual Desktop Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a CVE-verified Veteran-Owned Small Business (VOSB) and a proud ISO 9001:2015 certified organization specializing in systems integration and enterprise management solutions. As a trusted GSA MAS contract holder, they deliver mission-critical telecommunications, network infrastructure, and IT services to Federal, State, and Local Government agencies, the U.S. Military, and private-sector clients.

They are seeking a motivated career and customer-oriented Senior Virtual Desktop Infrastructure Engineer to join their team in Doral, FL. This position is on-site.

Employment - Full time

Worksite Type: Onsite

Compensation: $ 130 – 138 K per year.

Security Clearance Required: Active Secret clearance required. Must be eligible to obtain and maintain Top Secret/SCI clearance.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

 - Lead engineering and modernization efforts for Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) environments supporting DoD missions, driving requirements analysis, solution design, and lifecycle documentation in alignment with DoD 5000.02 processes.

 - Engineer, manage, and optimize enterprise VDI platforms VMware Horizon 2512, with hands-on expertise in image creation, application layering, profile management, and performance tuning.

 - Administer and enhance VDI components, including Dynamic Environment Manager (DEM), App Volumes, Instant Clone Pools, FSLogix for O365, and associated user experience optimization tools.

 - Troubleshoot complex VDI issues, including pool configuration changes, vCenter related desktop failures, image related defects, and performance degradation across distributed environments. Maintain and update VDI Gold Images, executing monthly patch cycles, application updates, and secure baseline enforcement to ensure consistent, mission‑ready desktop delivery.

 - Ensure technical integrity and compliance of all VDI engineering deliverables across ISO/IEC 15288 Technical, Enterprise, and Agreement process areas.

 - Develop and maintain engineering documentation, including VDI architecture diagrams, system design documents, engineering plans, requirements specifications, and interface control documents.

 - Ensure cybersecurity, regulatory, and configuration compliance, including STIG implementation, RMF artifact development, secure baseline management, and continuous monitoring of VDI systems.

Minimum Qualifications:

 - BA/BS in a field relevant to VDI engineering or systems engineering (additional experience may be substituted for degree requirements).
   
   - High School diploma plus 4 years of additional experience, or an Associate’s degree plus 2 years of additional experience, may substitute for a Bachelor’s degree.
   
   - A relevant Master’s degree may count for 2 years of experience; a relevant PhD may count for 4 years.

 - Five (5) or more years of systems engineering or VDI engineering experience.

 - Eight (8) or more years of information technology experience.

Desired Qualifications:

 - Hands‑on expertise with enterprise VDI platforms such as VMware Horizon (including Horizon 2512), ESXi, vCenter, and enterprise storage technologies.

 - Experience with cloud‑based or hybrid VDI solutions, systems modeling, automation frameworks, and enterprise monitoring/observability tools.

 - Familiarity with RMF, STIGs, secure baseline management, and cybersecurity compliance requirements for virtualized and distributed environments.

 - Working knowledge of adjacent technical domains such as networking, Active Directory, scripting/automation (PowerShell, Python), endpoint management, or identity services.

 - Experience with key VDI ecosystem components including Dynamic Environment Manager (DEM), App Volumes, Instant Clone Pools, FSLogix for O365, VDI Gold Image management, and troubleshooting vCenter‑related desktop issues.

 - Professional certifications such as VCP

source_jobs.title: Video Teleconferencing Technician
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a CVE-verified Veteran-Owned Small Business (VOSB) and a proud ISO 9001:2015 certified organization specializing in systems integration and enterprise management solutions. As a trusted GSA MAS contract holder, they deliver mission-critical telecommunications, network infrastructure, and IT services to Federal, State, and Local Government agencies, the U.S. Military, and private-sector clients.

They are seeking a Video Teleconferencing Technician to join their team in Doral, FL. This position is on-site. Must have an active Top Secret clearance.

Employment - Full time

Worksite Type: Onsite

Compensation: $80,000.00 - $83,000.00

Security Clearance Required: Must have an active Top Secret clearance.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

 - Tier I & II customer support

 - Scheduling conferences using Cisco’s TelePresence Management Suite (TMS)

 - Responding to system outages and taking appropriate action(s) to alt-route and/or use available failover/back-up capabilities to meet VTC client needs

 - Conducting site surveys both remotely and on-site to determine specific requirements for new VTC system installations and/or upgrades

 - Conducting on-site VTC installations to include assisting with end network device configurations, equipment installations, and equipment configuration and check-out

Minimum Qualifications:

 - Working / practical knowledge and understanding of IP networks and how they are implemented as part of / support of VTC systems

 - Providing Tier I & II customer support of VTC systems

Desired Qualifications:

 - Experience at a DoD Combatant Command (e.g., SOUTHCOM, NORTHCOM, CENTCOM, CYBERCOM, INDOPACOM, EUCOM, AFRICOM, STRATCOM, TRANSCOM, SOCOM, SPACECOM) or a component is desired.

Physical Requirements:

 - Must be able to remain in a stationary position 50%.

 - Constantly operates a computer and other office productivity machinery, such as a calculator, copy machine and computer printer.

 - The person in this position frequently communicates with co-workers, management and customers, which may involve delivering presentations. Must be able to exchange accurate information in these situations.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 10 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client also considers all qualified applicants for employment without regard to disability or veteran status or any other status protected under any federal, state, or local law or regulation.

As a trusted GSA MAS contract holder, they deliver mission-critical telecommunications, network infrastructure, and IT services to Federal, State, and Local Government agencies, the U.S. Military, and private-sector clients.

They are seeking a motivated career and customer-oriented Voice and Video Engineer to join their team in Doral, FL. This position is on-site. Must have an active Top Secret clearance with SCI eligibility.

Employment - Full time

Worksite Type: Onsite

Compensation: $130K – 132K per year.

Security Clearance Required: Must have an active Top Secret clearance with SCI Eligibility.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

 - Possesses in-depth knowledge of VoIP, video conferencing, unified communications platforms, and network infrastructure.

 - Must be able to communicate, converse, and exchange information with peers and senior personnel

source_jobs.title: Site Superintendent
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. . Our client is prominent vendor for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), frequently winning contracts for emergency generator repairs, campus infrastructure upgrades, and facility maintenance.

They are seeking an experienced Site Superintendent to oversee all onsite construction activities for commercial and federal construction projects in Brooklyn, NY. The Superintendent serves as the highest-ranking field manager and is responsible for overall project execution, production, quality control, safety, subcontractor coordination, and schedule management.

The successful candidate will maintain a continuous onsite presence, ensure compliance with project specifications, drawings, and applicable codes, and work closely with Project Managers, Owners, Architects, Engineers, and Quality Control personnel to successfully deliver projects on schedule and within budget.

Essential Responsibilities:

· Direct and supervise all daily field operations from project mobilization through closeout.

· Coordinate and oversee subcontractors, suppliers, inspectors, and field personnel.

· Maintain project schedules and monitor production to ensure milestones are achieved.

· Ensure all work complies with contract documents, specifications, applicable codes, and client requirements.

· Lead daily coordination meetings with subcontractors and project staff.

· Review construction drawings, specifications, RFIs, submittals, and change orders.

· Coordinate inspections with owners, architects, engineers, and government representatives.

· Work closely with the Contractor Quality Control (CQC) Manager to ensure implementation of the three-phase quality control process:

· Preparatory Phase

· Initial Phase

· Follow-Up Phase

· Ensure deficiencies are corrected promptly and documented appropriately.

· Monitor quality of workmanship throughout all phases of construction.

· Coordinate material deliveries, equipment utilization, and site logistics.

· Maintain daily construction reports documenting manpower, weather, equipment, production, inspections, delays, and safety activities.

· Oversee punch list completion and project closeout activities.

· Maintain project documentation in accordance with contract requirements.

· Ensure jobsite security, housekeeping, and organization at all times.

Safety Responsibilities:

· Promote a culture of safety on every project.

· Enforce OSHA regulations and company safety policies.

· Conduct daily safety meetings and toolbox talks.

· Coordinate Activity Hazard Analyses (AHAs) prior to beginning new work activities.

· Ensure all personnel utilize required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).

· Work closely with the Site Safety & Health Officer to maintain a safe work environment.

Required Qualifications:

· High School Diploma or GED required.

· Associate's or Bachelor's Degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, or related field preferred.

· Minimum 7 years of commercial construction experience.

· Minimum 5 years serving as a Superintendent managing commercial, institutional, or federal construction projects.

· Experience supervising multiple subcontractors simultaneously.

· Strong knowledge of construction sequencing, scheduling, and quality control procedures.

· Ability to interpret construction drawings, specifications, contracts, and technical documents.

· Knowledge of New York City Building Codes and construction regulations.

· Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills.

· Strong problem-solving and decision-making abilities.

· Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite.

· Experience with Procore, Autodesk Build, Bluebeam, Primavera P6, Microsoft Project, or similar construction management software.

Preferred Qualifications:

· OSHA 30-Hour Construction Certification.

· First Aid/CPR Certification.

· Experience with federal construction projects.

· E

source_jobs.title: Quality Control Manager
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is prominent vendor for the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), frequently winning contracts for emergency generator repairs, campus infrastructure upgrades, and facility maintenance.

They are seeking an experienced Quality Control Manager (QCM) to oversee the Contractor Quality Control (CQC) program for a federal construction project in Brooklyn, NY. The Quality Control Manager will be responsible for implementing and managing the project's Quality Control Plan, ensuring all construction activities comply with contract specifications, federal regulations, applicable building codes, and quality standards.

This individual serves as the primary point of contact for all quality-related matters and works closely with project management, subcontractors, government representatives, and inspection agencies to deliver a successful project.

Responsibilities:

· Develop, implement, and maintain the Contractor Quality Control (CQC) Plan in accordance with project requirements and FAR 52.246-12, Inspection of Construction.

· Manage and administer the Three-Phase Quality Control System, including Preparatory, Initial, and Follow-Up inspections for all definable features of work.

· Conduct daily quality inspections to verify compliance with contract documents, specifications, approved submittals, and applicable codes.

· Coordinate quality control activities with project management, subcontractors, testing laboratories, architects, engineers, and government representatives.

· Review construction drawings, specifications, shop drawings, RFIs, product data, and material submittals for compliance.

· Verify that materials, equipment, and workmanship meet project requirements before installation.

· Monitor subcontractor performance and ensure compliance with approved quality standards.

· Coordinate all required testing and inspections, including independent testing agencies and Special Inspections.

· Identify quality deficiencies, issue corrective actions, and verify resolution prior to continuation of work.

· Maintain complete project quality documentation, including daily reports, inspection records, testing logs, deficiency tracking, and quality control reports.

· Conduct punch list inspections, pre-final inspections, and final quality inspections.

· Participate in preconstruction meetings, coordination meetings, owner meetings, and project progress meetings.

· Maintain effective communication with the Owner, Contracting Officer, project team, and regulatory agencies.

· Ensure quality activities are properly documented throughout the life of the project.

Required Qualifications:

· Bachelor's degree in Construction Management, Civil Engineering, Architecture, or a related technical field, or equivalent construction experience.

· Minimum 5 years of construction quality control experience on commercial, institutional, healthcare, or federal construction projects.

·Thorough knowledge of Contractor Quality Control (CQC) procedures and federal construction quality standards.

· Experience administering the Three-Phase Control System.

· Strong understanding of construction methods, contract documents, specifications, and quality assurance processes.

· Experience coordinating inspections, testing agencies, and project closeout documentation.

· Ability to read and interpret construction drawings, specifications, and engineering documents.

· Strong organizational, leadership, and communication skills.

· Proficiency with Microsoft Office and construction management software.

· Preferred Qualifications

· Experience managing quality control on Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), USACE, NAVFAC, or other federal construction projects.

· OSHA 30-Hour Construction Certification.

· Experience with Procore, Bluebeam, Autodesk Construction Cloud, or similar project management platforms.

· Certified Construction Mana

source_jobs.title: Engineering Technician 4
source_jobs.description:
SGP Recruiting provides both operations and strategic support to Tribal 8(a) and commercial organizations. Our client is a family of SBA-certified Native American Tribal 8(a) and multi-certified companies delivering IT, management consulting, and general construction solutions to the Federal Government and commercial sector. They are actively seeking an Engineering Technician 4 based in Colorado Springs, CO. This is a full-time, onsite position. This position requires an active Top Secret clearance with the ability to obtain Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI).

This is an exciting opportunity to be part of our client’s team supporting a Space Command program in Colorado Springs, CO. The main responsibilities of the role include:

· Engineering Technician 4 position will be supporting the buildup and modernization of facilities across Colorado Springs.

· You will be part of a 10-15 -member team that works closely with the customer and other program teams to deliver quality systems.

· The Network Cabling Technician / Engineering Technician 4 works on a team supporting cabling, equipment and C4ISR system installations. This position pulls, terminates, and dresses Category 6A and Fiber Optic cabling. Installs and documents IT equipment, cable conveyance, antennas and supporting systems.

· Performs assignments that are not completely standardized or prescribed. Selects or adapts standard procedures or equipment, using fully applicable precedents.

· The Engineering Technician 4 receives initial instructions, equipment requirements, and advice from supervisor or engineer as needed; performs recurring work independently; work is reviewed for technical adequacy or conformity with instructions.

In this role you will be working in clearance required facilities:

· Contribute to all phases of the infrastructure installation or upgrade.

· Pulling and dressing of fiber optic and copper cables.

· Installation of racks/credenzas and all peripheral equipment.

· Rack and stacking of equipment in racks/credenzas.

· Termination of copper cable up to CAT6A and SM/MM Fiber single strand to MPO/MTP.

· Solving issues related to cables and networks.

· Testing and recording of all cables.

· Installation per a SIPH standard IDP, adapt/troubleshoot/modify where necessary, and provide redlines back to an AutoCAD drafter for As-built updates.

· Resolve technical/physical issues often requiring coordination with other disciplines: networking, facilities, HVAC, electrical, security, etc.

· Conduct and document PITCO and SOVT activities.

· Train other team members or subcontract staff as required to accomplish work activities.



Clearance Requirement:

· Technicians will be required to obtain SCI Government Security Clearance, most currently have Top Secret Clearance.



Qualifications:

· BICSI certification required.

· 5 years of related experience.

Our client provides a variety of benefits including company-paid health, dental & vision insurance coverage, as well as additional employee-paid health insurance options; company-paid life and disability insurance; 401k retirement savings plan with employer match; 11 company paid holidays per year, and paid time off.

Our client is committed to hiring and retaining a diverse workforce. They are proud to be an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer, making decisions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class. They are committed to a collaborative, inclusive environment that encourages authenticity and fosters a sense of belonging. We are also committed to providing accommodations for persons with disabilities.

notes: Multiple job postings require active clearances, indicating a strong presence in the government contracting space.
